title: 10 best herbal teas for acid reflux
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acid reflux are caffeine-free, plant-based beverages that can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux by reducing stomach acid and inflammation. Examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for acid reflux include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, while ginger tea can help reduce nausea and inflammation. Licorice root tea can help increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, protecting it from stomach acid. Marshmallow root tea can also help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with acid reflux can find relief from their symptoms, allowing them to enjoy their meals and improve their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with acid reflux because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. It can also help relieve symptoms of acid reflux by promoting the production of digestive juices and bile, which can improve overall digestion and help prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. Additionally, lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. Drinking lemon balm tea regularly may help alleviate acid reflux and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to its rosmarinic acid content.
Spasmolytic: It helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, easing the movement of food and reducing acid reflux, thanks to its eugenol and citral content.
Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, thus reducing the likelihood of acid reflux, due to its volatile oils and tannins.
Antacid: Lemon balm tea can neutralize stomach acid, providing immediate relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to its mildly basic nature.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea can reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger acid reflux, due to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their antispasmodic and calming properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of acid reflux.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are sometimes included in medicinal tea for acid reflux as they contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are not typically used in medicinal tea for acid reflux, as they contain essential oils that can be irritating to the digestive tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with acid reflux because it contains substances that can help balance the stomach's acid production. One of its key components, glycyrrhizic acid, can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. Moreover, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. By promoting the production of mucus and reducing inflammation, licorice tea can alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux and provide relief for those who suffer from this condition.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tea forms a protective film on the mucous membranes lining the esophagus, preventing irritation and damage caused by stomach acid, thus reducing acid reflux symptoms. This property is due to the high mucilage content in licorice root.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with acid reflux. The active compound glycyrrhizin is responsible for this anti-inflammatory effect.

Mucosal healing: Licorice tea promotes the healing of damaged esophageal tissue, facilitating faster recovery from acid reflux. The flavonoids and volatile oils in licorice root contribute to this healing process.

Stomach acid reduction: Licorice tea helps regulate stomach acid production, preventing excessive acidity that can lead to acid reflux. The compound glycyrrhizin is responsible for this property, as it inhibits the production of gastric acid.

Gastroprotective: Licorice tea shields the stomach lining from harmful digestive secretions, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. This property is attributed to the antioxidant and free radical scavenging abilities of the flavonoids and coumarins found in licorice root.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help balance stomach acid levels and soothe inflammation in the esophagus.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are rich in flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating acid reflux.
Bark: The bark of the licorice tea plant contains compounds that can help stimulate the production of mucus in the stomach, providing a protective barrier against acid.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain essential oils that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with acid reflux because it has several properties that can soothe and protect the digestive tract. It contains volatile oils, such as anethole, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms, thereby alleviating the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, fennel tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and protect it from damage caused by acid reflux. Furthermore, fennel tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and reduce the likelihood of acid reflux. Overall, fennel tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and limonene.
Carminative: Fennel tea's carminative properties help relieve gas and bloating, which can contribute to acid reflux. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils and rosmarinic acid.

Antispasmodic: Fennel tea's antispasmodic properties help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing muscle spasms that can cause acid reflux. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, particularly anethole and fenchone.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea's ability to stimulate digestion helps improve the overall functioning of the digestive system, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils and rosmarinic acid.

Demulcent: Fennel tea's demulcent properties help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, reducing irritation caused by acid reflux. This is due to the presence of mucilage, a type of soluble fiber found in fennel.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas, bloating, and other digestive discomforts.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant are used in tea to soothe inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Stems: Fennel stems contain volatile oils that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are used in tea to aid digestion, reduce gastric spasms, and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common agrimony tea helps with acid reflux because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. These tannins can help strengthen the muscles in the esophagus, allowing it to close more effectively and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up. Additionally, agrimony has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, helping to break down food more efficiently and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. The tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Agrimony tea is rich in tannins, which have an astringent effect that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in agrimony tea, such as luteolin and apigenin, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate acid reflux.
Mucilage content: Agrimony tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the mucous membranes, which can help prevent acid and digestive enzymes from irritating the esophagus.

Bitterness-inducing properties: Agrimony tea has a slightly bitter taste due to its bitter glycosides, which can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.

Antispasmodic properties: Agrimony tea contains antispasmodic compounds, such as catechins and epicatechins, that can help relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the agrimony plant are commonly used in tea due to their tannin content, which can help reduce acid production in the stomach and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the agrimony plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of the agrimony plant still contain tannins and can contribute to the overall effectiveness of the tea in treating acid reflux.
Roots: The roots of the agrimony plant contain high levels of tannins and have astringent properties, making them useful for reducing acid production and inflammation in the digestive tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with acid reflux because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. The tea is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, aiding in proper digestion and preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. Additionally, pot marigold tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux occurring. Its soothing and calming effects on the digestive system make it an ideal natural remedy for acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and carotenoids.

Astringent: Pot marigold tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the tissues of the esophagus, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Antispasmodic: Pot marigold tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent acid reflux caused by muscle spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Demulcent: Pot marigold tea has demulcent properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by exposure to stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include mucilage and flavonoids.

Digestive: Pot marigold tea has digestive properties that can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent acid reflux caused by poor digestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include bitter principles and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the esophagus.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are often used in tea for acid reflux because they contain mucilage, which can protect the stomach lining.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation, making them a useful addition to medicinal tea for acid reflux.
Stems: The stems of the pot marigold plant contain compounds that can help reduce stomach acid production, making them a valuable component in tea for acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with acid reflux because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. The tea is also known for its natural calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to prevent the occurrence of acid reflux. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help to repair damaged tissue in the esophagus and stomach, further reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. The tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids such as quercetin and luteolin.

Mucilaginous: The mucilaginous property of large-leaved lime tea creates a protective barrier in the stomach and esophagus, preventing acid from irritating the lining. This soothing effect is attributed to pectins and other polysaccharides found in the tea.
Astringent: Large-leaved lime tea has astringent properties that help tone and strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux. This is due to the tannins present in the tea.

Antacid: Large-leaved lime tea possesses antacid properties that neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from acid reflux symptoms. The antacid effect can be attributed to the alkaloids and flavonoids present in the tea.

Carminative: Large-leaved lime tea has carminative properties that help relieve gas and bloating, common symptoms associated with acid reflux. This is due to the volatile oils and terpenes found in the tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, helping to soothe irritation in the esophagus and alleviate acid reflux.
Bark: Has tannins that can help reduce inflammation and protect the esophageal lining, providing relief from acid reflux.
Seeds: Contain essential oils that can help stimulate digestion, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with acid reflux because it contains high levels of mucilage, a substance that creates a soothing film over the mucous membranes lining the stomach and esophagus. This film can help to protect the digestive tract from acid and reduce inflammation, providing relief from the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, comfrey tea has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that can help to reduce the production of stomach acid and alleviate discomfort. Furthermore, it has been suggested that comfrey tea may help to speed up the healing process of damaged tissue in the digestive tract, providing long-term relief from acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Mucilaginous: The mucilage in comfrey tea coats and soothes the digestive tract lining, protecting it from acid and irritation.
Astringent: Tannins present in comfrey tea help tighten and strengthen tissue, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux by improving esophageal sphincter function.
Demulcent: Comfrey tea's soothing and healing properties help restore the damaged mucous membrane in the esophagus, promoting faster recovery from acid reflux.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in comfrey tea, such as rosmarinic acid, protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the digestive lining, providing relief for acid reflux symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey rhyzomes contain allantoin, a substance that supports gut healing and reduces inflammation, making it beneficial for acid reflux treatment.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers are a source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, aiding in the reduction of acid reflux-related irritation and inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with acid reflux because it contains a mucilaginous substance that can create a protective barrier over the lining of the esophagus. This barrier can help prevent stomach acid from irritating and damaging the esophagus, thereby reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating discomfort. The tea is also believed to have a calming effect on the stomach, which can help reduce the production of stomach acid and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes the mouth, throat, and stomach, protecting them from irritation and providing relief from acid reflux.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Mucoprotective property: Marshmallow tea's mucilage forms a protective layer over the mucous membranes, shielding them from damaging stomach acids and reducing acid reflux symptoms.
Emollient property: The emollient effect of marshmallow tea helps maintain the integrity of the gastrointestinal lining, preventing the backflow of stomach acids and easing acid reflux.
Soothing property: The soothing effect of marshmallow tea on the digestive system helps alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by reducing irritation and promoting overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow tea plant are rich in mucilage, which can help to coat and soothe the esophagus, reducing the symptoms of acid reflux.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow tea plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate acid reflux.
Roots: The roots of the marshmallow tea plant are a demulcent, meaning they can help to soothe and protect mucous membranes, providing relief from acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow tea plant contain high levels of mucilage, which can help to form a protective barrier in the stomach, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. Its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties can also help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to acid reflux. Additionally, oregano tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help prevent acid reflux. Furthermore, its soothing and calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active compounds like rosmarinic acid and carvacrol, can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of oregano tea, from active constituents like thymol and carvacrol, can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the gut that can contribute to acid reflux.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of oregano tea, from compounds like rosmarinic acid and thymol, can help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the risk of acid reflux.

Digestive aid: Oregano tea's ability to aid digestion, due to active constituents like carvacrol and thymol, can help improve the breakdown and absorption of food, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.
Pain relief: The pain-relieving properties of oregano tea, from active compounds like carvacrol and thymol, can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tea plant contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help soothe digestion and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds of the oregano tea plant are rich in vitamins and minerals that can help strengthen the digestive system and prevent acid reflux.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with acid reflux because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus. The rosmarinic acid found in rosemary has been shown to help reduce gastric acid production, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, rosemary tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and reduce the likelihood of acid reflux. Drinking rosemary tea after meals may help prevent acid reflux and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of compounds such as carnosol and carnosic acid.

Antispasmodic: Rosemary tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing acid reflux caused by muscle spasms. The active constituents responsible for this include volatile oils like cineole and camphor.

Carminative: Rosemary tea promotes healthy digestion by preventing the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract. This is particularly helpful for acid reflux, as excessive gas can put pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter, causing acid to leak back up into the esophagus. The tea's carminative properties are attributed to compounds like alpha-pinene and limonene.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect the mucosal lining of the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux. These antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, also support overall gut health.

Astringent: Rosemary tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone tissues in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. The tannins in rosemary tea contribute to its astringent effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain volatile oils and antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and improve digestion, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
Stems: May have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce acid reflux.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, they can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce acid reflux.
Seeds: Contain rosmarinic acid and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and acid production, providing relief from acid reflux.
